Rust and Marty interrogate Charlie Lange about his former cellmate Reggie Ledoux. Charlie reveals that Reggie discussed a place "down south where all these rich men go to devil-worship" involving sacrificing women and children. He references "Carcosa" and "the Yellow King" and describes a spiral brand on Reggie's back as "their sign." Charlie also admits he showed Reggie explicit Polaroids of Dora that she had sent him in prison.

Marty tracks Tyrone Weems to a warehouse rave and violently confronts him for information about Reggie. Tyrone reveals that Reggie now cooks exclusively for a biker gang called the Iron Crusaders.

Cohle devises an off-the-books plan. Having previously spent four years undercover with biker gangs, he has contacts among the Iron Crusaders. He steals cocaine from the police evidence room to use as bait and reconnects with his former contact Ginger, posing as a former cartel security man looking to set up his own operation. Ginger agrees to connect Cohle with Ledoux's supplier, but only if Cohle helps rob a drug stash house in a housing project that night.

The episode's centerpiece is a legendary six-minute single-take sequence: Cohle enters the stash house with the bikers disguised as cops, steals drugs from a hidden wall panel, and a shootout erupts. As the entire neighborhood descends into chaos, Cohle kidnaps Ginger and fights his way through houses, across lawns, and past arriving police, finally leaping into Hart's getaway car at the last moment.

Meanwhile, Marty's affair with Lisa is exposed to Maggie, and his marriage unravels. He displays aggressive behavior toward both women, refusing accountability and blaming his father's death and alcohol.

In 2012, Papania and Gilbough question why Cohle took two weeks' leave at a critical moment in the investigation. Both Cohle and Hart claim Cohle visited his sick father, but the detectives note there are no hospital records to support this.
